───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 10: GENERAL PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it 
     has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two 
     years from the issue date.

10.2 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE OR SEX: If the age or sex of the 
     insured has been misstated, we will adjust the death 
     benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased 
     at the correct age

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 10: GENERAL PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it 
     has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two 
     years from the issue date.

10.2 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE OR SEX: If the age or sex of the 
     insured has been misstated, we will adjust the death 
     benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased 
     at the correct age

SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 10: GENERAL PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications.     refund of premiums paid.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

results.question: If death occurs after the policy has been in force for three years due to a cause other than suicide, will the insurer be able to contest the claim on the basis of misrepresentation in the application?
results.expected_answer: No. The incontestability clause prevents the insurer from contesting the policy after two years in force during the insured's lifetime, and suicide is the only stated exclusion.
results.evidence: 10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date. / 7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.
results.llm_answer: Yes, the insurer will not be able to contest the claim on the basis of misrepresentation if the death occurs after three years, as the policy is still in force and the death benefit is payable upon death during the policy term, provided all conditions are met.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    policy without evidence of insurability.

    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted

This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. 9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

    refund of premiums paid.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

     benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased 
     at the correct age

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 10: GENERAL PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it 
     has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two 
     years from the issue date.

10.2 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE OR SEX: If the age or sex of the 
     insured has been misstated, we will adjust the death 
     benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased 
     at the correct age

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

                    LIFE INSURANCE POLICY
                    SecureLife Insurance Company
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

POLICY NUMBER: SL-2024-TRM-00123
ISSUE DATE: January 15, 2024
POLICY TYPE: 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 1: POLICY OWNER AND INSURED
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Policy Owner: John Michael Anderson
Insured Person: John Michael Anderson
Date of Birth: March 22, 1985
Age at Issue: 38 years

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 2: COVERAGE DETAILS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Death Benefit Amount: $500,000
Policy Term: 20 years (expires January 15, 2044)
Premium Amount: $45.00 per month
Premium Payment Mode: Monthly automatic deduction

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 3: BENEFICIARY DESIGNATION
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Primary Beneficiary: Sarah Elizabeth Anderson (Spouse) - 100%
Contingent Beneficiary: Emma Grace Anderson (Daughter) - 50%
                        Lucas James Anderson (Son) - 50%

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 4: DEFINITIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

4.1 "Death Benefit" means the amount payable to the beneficiary 
    upon the death of the insured during the policy term.

4.2 "Premium Due Date" means the 15th day of each month.

4.3 "Grace Period" means the 31-day period following the premium 
    due date during which coverage continues without lapse.

4.4 "Policy Anniversary" means the same day and month as the 
    issue date in each subsequent year.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 5: DEATH BENEFIT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

5.1 We will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if:
    (a) The insured dies while this policy is in force, and
    (b) We receive proof of death acceptable to us, and
    (c) The death occurs before the policy expiry date.

5.2 The death benefit will be paid in one lump sum unless the 
    beneficiary chooses an alternate settlement option.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS

1| the policy expiry date.

5.2 The death benefit will be paid in one lump sum unless the 
    beneficiary chooses an alternate settlement option.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

0|SECURELIFE INSURANCE COMPANY|SecureLife Insurance Company is the primary insurer and issuing organization responsible for the administration, underwriting, and management of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy. As the issuing entity, SecureLife Insurance Company oversees the entire lifecycle of the policy, including the initial underwriting process, policy issuance, and ongoing management of policy terms. The company is tasked with collecting premium payments from policyholders in accordance with the agreed-upon schedule and ensuring that coverage remains active throughout the 20-year term. 

SecureLife Insurance Company also manages key policy features such as adjustments to death benefits, which may be made under specific conditions and with proper documentation, and facilitates policy assignments, allowing policyholders to transfer ownership or rights under the policy to another party when permitted by law and policy terms. The company is responsible for processing and adjudicating claims upon the death of the insured, ensuring that the designated beneficiaries receive the death benefit in a timely and accurate manner, provided the claim is valid and the insured passed away during the policy’s active term.

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications. Its role is critical in maintaining the financial and operational stability of the policy, providing beneficiaries with assurance that the promised death benefit will be paid when due. Through its structured processes and regulatory compliance, SecureLife Insurance Company upholds its commitment to policyholder protection and service excellence.|13

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 8: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

8.1 You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance 
    policy without evidence of insurability.

8.2 Conversion must occur before the earlier of:
    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

8.3 The premium for the new policy will be based on:
    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 9: CLAIMS PROCEDURE
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

9.1 To claim the death benefit, the beneficiary must submit:
    (a) Completed claim form
    (b) Certified copy of the death certificate
    (c) Original policy document (if available)

9.2 Claims should be sent to:
    SecureLife Insurance Company
    Claims Department
    P.O. Box 5000, Hartford, CT 06102

9.3 We will pay valid claims within 30 days of receiving all 
    required documentation.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 10: GENERAL PROVISIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10.1 INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it 
     has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two 
     years from the issue date.

10.2 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE OR SEX: If the age or sex of the 
     insured has been misstated, we will adjust the death 
     benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased 
     at the correct age

As the issuing entity, SecureLife Insurance Company oversees the entire lifecycle of the policy, including the initial underwriting process, policy issuance, and ongoing management of policy terms. The company is tasked with collecting premium payments from policyholders in accordance with the agreed-upon schedule and ensuring that coverage remains active throughout the 20-year term. 

SecureLife Insurance Company also manages key policy features such as adjustments to death benefits, which may be made under specific conditions and with proper documentation, and facilitates policy assignments, allowing policyholders to transfer ownership or rights under the policy to another party when permitted by law and policy terms. The company is responsible for processing and adjudicating claims upon the death of the insured, ensuring that the designated beneficiaries receive the death benefit in a timely and accurate manner, provided the claim is valid and the insured passed away during the policy’s active term.

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications. 0|SECURELIFE INSURANCE COMPANY|SecureLife Insurance Company is the primary insurer and issuing organization responsible for the administration, underwriting, and management of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy. As the issuing entity, SecureLife Insurance Company oversees the entire lifecycle of the policy, including the initial underwriting process, policy issuance, and ongoing management of policy terms. The company is tasked with collecting premium payments from policyholders in accordance with the agreed-upon schedule and ensuring that coverage remains active throughout the 20-year term. 

SecureLife Insurance Company also manages key policy features such as adjustments to death benefits, which may be made under specific conditions and with proper documentation, and facilitates policy assignments, allowing policyholders to transfer ownership or rights under the policy to another party when permitted by law and policy terms. The company is responsible for processing and adjudicating claims upon the death of the insured, ensuring that the designated beneficiaries receive the death benefit in a timely and accurate manner, provided the claim is valid and the insured passed away during the policy’s active term.

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. 9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

    (a) The policy expiry date, or
    (b) The insured's 65th birthday.

    (a) The insured's attained age at conversion
    (b) The amount of coverage converted
    (c) The class of permanent policy selected

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 1: POLICY OWNER AND INSURED
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Policy Owner: John Michael Anderson
Insured Person: John Michael Anderson
Date of Birth: March 22, 1985
Age at Issue: 38 years

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 2: COVERAGE DETAILS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Death Benefit Amount: $500,000
Policy Term: 20 years (expires January 15, 2044)
Premium Amount: $45.00 per month
Premium Payment Mode: Monthly automatic deduction

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 3: BENEFICIARY DESIGNATION
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Primary Beneficiary: Sarah Elizabeth Anderson (Spouse) - 100%
Contingent Beneficiary: Emma Grace Anderson (Daughter) - 50%
                        Lucas James Anderson (Son) - 50%

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 4: DEFINITIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

4.1 "Death Benefit" means the amount payable to the beneficiary 
    upon the death of the insured during the policy term.

4.2 "Premium Due Date" means the 15th day of each month.

4.3 "Grace Period" means the 31-day period following the premium 
    due date during which coverage continues without lapse.

4.4 "Policy Anniversary" means the same day and month as the 
    issue date in each subsequent year.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 5: DEATH BENEFIT
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

5.1 We will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if:
    (a) The insured dies while this policy is in force, and
    (b) We receive proof of death acceptable to us, and
    (c) The death occurs before the policy expiry date.

5.2 The death benefit will be paid in one lump sum unless the 
    beneficiary chooses an alternate settlement option.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 6: PREMIUM PAYMENTS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6.1 Premiums are due monthly on the 15th of each month.

6.2 If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 
    31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.

6.3 If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, 
    the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SECTION 7: EXCLUSIONS
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

7.1 SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two 
    years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a 
    refund of premiums paid.

7.2 No other exclusions apply to this policy.

0|SECURELIFE INSURANCE COMPANY|SecureLife Insurance Company is the primary insurer and issuing organization responsible for the administration, underwriting, and management of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy. As the issuing entity, SecureLife Insurance Company oversees the entire lifecycle of the policy, including the initial underwriting process, policy issuance, and ongoing management of policy terms. The company is tasked with collecting premium payments from policyholders in accordance with the agreed-upon schedule and ensuring that coverage remains active throughout the 20-year term. 

SecureLife Insurance Company also manages key policy features such as adjustments to death benefits, which may be made under specific conditions and with proper documentation, and facilitates policy assignments, allowing policyholders to transfer ownership or rights under the policy to another party when permitted by law and policy terms. The company is responsible for processing and adjudicating claims upon the death of the insured, ensuring that the designated beneficiaries receive the death benefit in a timely and accurate manner, provided the claim is valid and the insured passed away during the policy’s active term.

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications. Its role is critical in maintaining the financial and operational stability of the policy, providing beneficiaries with assurance that the promised death benefit will be paid when due. Through its structured processes and regulatory compliance, SecureLife Insurance Company upholds its commitment to policyholder protection and service excellence.|13
9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

9|POLICY EXPIRY DATE|The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is January 15, 2044, which marks the conclusion of the 20-year term of the policy. On this date, the policy will terminate, and no death benefit will be payable, regardless of the insured’s health status or life expectancy at the time. This expiration date serves as a critical deadline for the insured, as it necessitates action prior to the end of the term. Specifically, the insured must convert the policy into a permanent life insurance policy before the expiry date to maintain coverage. Importantly, this conversion must be completed without providing evidence of insurability, meaning the insured will not be required to undergo medical underwriting or provide health-related documentation. Failure to convert the policy by January 15, 2044, will result in the policy lapsing, thereby terminating all coverage and benefits. The POLICY EXPIRY DATE is thus both a terminus for the current term and a pivotal opportunity for the insured to secure long-term protection through conversion. This provision ensures that individuals with changing insurance needs can transition to permanent coverage while still eligible, preserving their ability to obtain life insurance even if their health deteriorates over time. The date is fixed and non-negotiable, underscoring the importance of timely planning and awareness of the policy’s timeline. In summary, the POLICY EXPIRY DATE of January 15, 2044, is a definitive endpoint for the term policy, after which no benefits will be paid, and a final window for conversion to permanent insurance without medical evidence closes.|5

0|SECURELIFE INSURANCE COMPANY|SecureLife Insurance Company is the primary insurer and issuing organization responsible for the administration, underwriting, and management of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy. As the issuing entity, SecureLife Insurance Company oversees the entire lifecycle of the policy, including the initial underwriting process, policy issuance, and ongoing management of policy terms. The company is tasked with collecting premium payments from policyholders in accordance with the agreed-upon schedule and ensuring that coverage remains active throughout the 20-year term. 

SecureLife Insurance Company also manages key policy features such as adjustments to death benefits, which may be made under specific conditions and with proper documentation, and facilitates policy assignments, allowing policyholders to transfer ownership or rights under the policy to another party when permitted by law and policy terms. The company is responsible for processing and adjudicating claims upon the death of the insured, ensuring that the designated beneficiaries receive the death benefit in a timely and accurate manner, provided the claim is valid and the insured passed away during the policy’s active term.

In addition to its role in issuing and administering the policy, SecureLife Insurance Company maintains the integrity of the policy’s terms and conditions, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines. The company’s responsibilities extend to managing policy conversions, enabling eligible policyholders to convert their term policy into a permanent life insurance product, subject to underwriting and eligibility criteria. This comprehensive oversight ensures that policyholders receive consistent, reliable service throughout the duration of their coverage.

Overall, SecureLife Insurance Company serves as the central authority for all aspects of the 20-Year Level Term Life Insurance policy, from initial issuance and premium collection to claims processing and policy modifications. Its role is critical in maintaining the financial and operational stability of the policy, providing beneficiaries with assurance that the promised death benefit will be paid when due. Through its structured processes and regulatory compliance, SecureLife Insurance Company upholds its commitment to policyholder protection and service excellence.|13
